---
title: Structs and methods
description: Use structs, methods, arrays, closures, and iterator helpers in Silex.
---

## Structs, methods, arrays, and maps

[Open in Playground](https://playground.xelis.io/?code=c3RydWN0IFRhc2sgewogICAgaWQ6IHU2NCwKICAgIHRpdGxlOiBzdHJpbmcsCiAgICBkb25lOiBib29sCn0KCnN0cnVjdCBUYXNrU3VtbWFyeVJwY0V2ZW50IHsKICAgIHRhc2s6IFRhc2ssCiAgICBub3JtYWxpemVkX3RhZ3M6IHN0cmluZ1tdCn0KCmZuICh0YXNrIFRhc2spIG1hcmtfZG9uZSgpIC0%)

Structs are useful for typed return data and event payloads. Methods are declared with the receiver before the function name.

```rust filename="task_summary.slx" copy
struct Task {
    id: u64,
    title: string,
    done: bool
}

struct TaskSummaryRpcEvent {
    task: Task,
    normalized_tags: string[]
}

fn (task Task) mark_done() -> Task {
    return Task {
        id: task.id,
        title: task.title,
        done: true
    }
}

entry summarize(title: string, tags: string[]) -> Task {
    let normalized: string[] = tags
        .iter()
        .map(|tag: string| { return tag.trim().to_lowercase() })
        .filter(|tag: string| { return !tag.is_empty() })
        .collect()

    let task: Task = Task {
        id: normalized.len() as u64,
        title,
        done: false
    }

    fire_rpc_event(2, TaskSummaryRpcEvent {
        task,
        normalized_tags: normalized
    })
    return task.mark_done()
}
```

This example also introduces array iteration, closures, string helpers, field shorthand, casts, and mixed event data.
